ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: How often do you experience problems with the speed, reliability, or quality of your high-speed internet connection at home in a way that makes it hard to do the things you need to do online?
Notes: 0= I do not have internet access at home; 1= Never; 2= Rarely; 3= Sometimes; 4= Often; -99= NA/RF

ElementDescription: What is the main reason for not having internet access at home?
Notes: 1= The monthly cost is too expensive; 2= I can do everything I need using my phone; 3= Internet service is not available where I live (or not available at an acceptable speed); 4= I have other options outside my home; 5= The cost of a computer is too expensive; 6= Other reason; -99= NA/RF
